message.content:
Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_17-35-01_game-of-life-prose_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/game-of-life.spec.ts
Failing test: "should bring a dead cell to life when it has exactly three live neighbors (reproduction)"
Expected behavior: A dead cell with exactly 3 live neighbors becomes alive in next generation
Current error: expected [ '1,1' ] to include '1,0'
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_17-35-01_game-of-life-prose_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/game-of-life.ts

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement minimal code to make it pass.

The current implementation only returns surviving cells but doesn't add dead cells that should come to life. I need to also check empty neighbor cells of live cells.

Test case: `["0,0", "2,0", "1,1"]` - cell `1,0` has neighbors `0,0`, `2,0`, `1,1` (3 live neighbors) → should become alive.

Minimal change: also iterate over dead neighbor cells and add them if they have exactly 3 live neighbors.

content.text:
All 7 tests pass (with 1 todo remaining).

Green Phase Complete:
- **Implementation**: Added a candidates set that collects neighbors of all live cells. After processing survivors, iterate through dead candidates and add those with exactly 3 live neighbors to the next generation.
- **Result**: All tests now pass (7 passing, 1 todo)
- **Approach**: This is minimal because the test demands that dead cells with 3 live neighbors come to life. The simplest way is to enumerate dead neighbor cells (the only candidates that could possibly have 3 live neighbors) and check each. Reused the existing `countLiveNeighbors` helper.
